channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Teacher and a teenage student killed in a shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A teenage student opened fire with a handgun Monday at a Christian school in Wisconsin, killing a teacher and another teenager during the final week before Christmas break. The shooter also died, police said.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Teacher and a student killed in a shooting at a private Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A teenage student opened fire at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin on Monday, killing a teacher and another teenage student in the final week before Christmas break. The shooter also died, police said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: 16-year-old killed entire family, called 911, police say
item.description: BELEN, N.M. (KRQE) – On Sunday, New Mexico State Police (NMSP) released the names of the four victims from Saturday's homicide in Belen. Police said that 16-year-old Diego Leyva called 911 early Saturday morning and told the dispatcher he had killed his family.
item.dc:creator: Scott Brown, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The US Census Bureau is adding refugees to its immigrant count
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau is changing how it counts immigrants in annual estimates by including more people who were admitted for humanitarian, and often temporary, reasons. The change is being made in an effort to better reflect population shifts this decade, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: California couple on vacation fatally shot in Mexico, officials say
item.description: An American couple from Northern California was shot and killed by gunmen in the western Mexican state of Michoacán, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Dogs involved in Mira Mesa mauling death euthanized
item.description: The XL bullies — a breed known for their size and strength — attacked their owner, prompting witnesses to call 911.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, KRON4

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 teens killed, 4 injured in shooting at makeshift club in Houston
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Two teens were killed and four were injured — including a 13-year-old girl — in a shooting at a makeshift club in Houston, police said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Ivy League professor addresses suspect's possible radicalization in college
item.description: A university professor explained how Ivy League campuses perpetuate radicalization, painting a picture of the UnitedHealthcare murder suspect's educational environment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Josh Allen has another huge game to lift Bills to a 48-42 victory that snaps Lions' winning streak
item.description: Allen ran for two scores in the first quarter and threw two touchdown passes in the second half, leading the Buffalo Bills to a 48-42 victory Sunday that snapped the Detroit Lions ' franchise-record 11-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of mushrooms, arsenal of guns, grenades seized from Oregon home
item.description: Charges are pending against 50-year-old Oregon resident Matthew Houglum and other suspected co-conspirators following the raid.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: 20 Pennsylvania residents charged for multi-million dollar Medicaid fraud
item.description: “The Medicaid program is designed to help vulnerable people in the Commonwealth, not line the pockets of those who deliberately exploit it,” Attorney General Henry said.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, NewsNation

item.title: Miami WR DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head against Texans
item.description: Miami receiver Grant DuBose was hospitalized in stable condition and undergoing further evaluation after being taken off the field on a stretcher following a helmet-to-helmet hit against the Houston Texans on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Grant DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head
item.description: Grant DuBose, who returned Sunday after a shoulder injury sidelined him for part of the season, was listed as being in stable condition.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Private Sin: How a church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about drones over New Jersey, other parts of the US
item.description: The drones have prompted concern from local residents, anger from lawmakers and endless speculation on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, ABC 4

item.title: Romney: ‘You can’t complain’ if Trump does what he says he would do
item.description: “Donald Trump won. He won overwhelmingly. He said what he was going to do, and that’s what he’s doing,” Romney said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Spotify too repetitive? Here's how to retrain the algorithm
item.description: Over the past couple years, complaints have piled up in online forums from music lovers having trouble discovering new music. Why? No matter what they do, Spotify seems to be serving them the same old songs, again and again.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: California man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, KTLA 5

item.title: Shiffrin recovering after abdominal surgery to clean out deep puncture wound suffered in race crash
item.description: Mikaela Shiffrin is recovering after undergoing abdominal surgery on Thursday night to clean out a puncture wound she received in a giant slalom crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: The original lyrics of 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas' were extremely depressing
item.description: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is already more melancholy than most classic Christmas songs, but its earlier version was a downright downer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: NJ drone sightings could be a 'classified exercise': former CIA officer
item.description: Former CIA officer Laura Ballman argues that drone sightings over New Jersey may be a testing operation by the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UK hockey team retires Adam Johnson's No. 47 to honor player who died after neck cut
item.description:
Adam Johnson made as big an impression off the ice as he did on it for the Nottingham Panthers.
The U.K. hockey team officially retired Johnson's No. 47 jersey on Saturday in tribute to the American forward who died after his neck was cut by an opponent’s skate during a game over a year ago.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, Fox 5
